Preface and acknowledgements Guidelines for Evaluating Water in Pit Slope Stability (the Frederic Donzé, Formerly CSIRO Earth Science and “Water Book’) is an outcome of the Large Open Pit (LOP) Resource Engineering, Brisbane, Australia project, an international research and technology transfer Greg Doubek, Doubek Hydrologic Inc., Denver, USA project on the stability of rock slopes in open pit mines. It Blair Douglas, BHPBilliton Iron Ore, Perth, Australia is a follow-up to Chapter 6 in the LOP project’s previous Jeremy Dowling, Schlumberger Water Services, Denver, publication Guidelines for Open Pit Slope Design (Read & USA Stacey, 2009), which outlined how the hydrogeological John De Souza, AngloGold Ashanti, Johannesburg, RSA model is developed and applied in the design of rock slopes. It describes the outcomes of hydrogeological Greg Ghidotti, RioTinto Group, Superior, Arizona, USA research performed by the LOP project since 2009 and has the objective of providing slope design practitioners with a Bart Harthong, Formerly CSIRO Earth Science and road map that that will help them decide how to Resource Engineering, Brisbane, Australia investigate and manage water pressures in pit slopes. Mark Hawley, Piteau Associates, Vancouver, Canada The LOP project was initiated in April 2005 and is Jim Hazard, Itasca Consulting Group, Inc., Minneapo- managed on behalf of CSIRO Australia by John Read, lis, USA CSIRO Earth Science and Resource Engineering, Brisbane, David Holmes, Schlumberger Water Services, Shrews- Australia.
